## SECTION 19 — This Day in History (Global)
*   In 1960, Theodore Maiman demonstrated the first working laser at Hughes Research Laboratories in Malibu, California.
*   In 1997, Mobutu Sese Seko, the long-time dictator of Zaire, fled Kinshasa as rebel forces advanced, leading to Laurent-Désiré Kabila taking power and renaming the country the Democratic Republic of Congo.
*   In 1943, the Dambusters Raid (Operation Chastise) by RAF No. 617 Squadron successfully attacked German dams using "bouncing bombs" during World War II.
*   In 1868, the U.S. Senate acquitted President Andrew Johnson of impeachment charges by one vote.
*   In 1920, Pope John Paul II was born Karol Józef Wojtyła in Wadowice, Poland.
*   In 1975, Junko Tabei became the first woman to reach the summit of Mount Everest.
*   In 1770, Marie Antoinette married Louis-Auguste, who would later become King Louis XVI of France.
*   In 1983, Michael Jackson first performed the "moonwalk" during his performance of "Billie Jean" on the TV special Motown 25: Yesterday, Today, Forever.

---

## SECTION 20 — This Day in History (India)
*   In 1975, Sikkim officially became the 22nd state of India.
*   In 1996, Atal Bihari Vajpayee was sworn in as the Prime Minister of India for the first time.
*   In 2014, the results of the Indian general election were declared, leading to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) winning a majority and Narendra Modi becoming Prime Minister.
*   In 1872, the first Indian railway tunnel was opened at Khandala on the Mumbai-Pune route.
*   In 1967, the first Indian-made nuclear reactor, CIRUS, became critical at Trombay.
*   In 1957, India issued its first decimal currency coins.
*   In 1905, the partition of Bengal was announced by Lord Curzon, leading to widespread protests.
